chunk_summary: Guidelines for inpatient stabilization of severe acute malnutrition (SAM) focus on cautious feeding, fluid management, and monitoring to prevent complications like heart failure and electrolyte imbalance.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of cautious feeding and fluid management in children with SAM.
Learn the use of F-75 starter feeds and reduced-osmolarity ORS in stabilization protocols.
Recognize emergency indications for IV fluids and their risks in SAM management.
Identify key monitoring parameters to detect early deterioration in SAM patients.
key_facts: Children with SAM and complications require stabilization before feeding due to impaired myocardial reserve and electrolyte handling.
Use reduced-osmolarity ORS with added potassium for rehydration, alternating with starter feeds, and monitor closely.
F-75 starter feeds should be given every 2–3 hours in small, weight-guided volumes; NG tubes are preferred if oral intake is insufficient.
Avoid adding salt or using diuretics for edema, as sodium load can worsen edema and heart failure risk.
IV fluids are reserved for emergencies like shock and must be reassessed frequently to avoid fluid overload.
reference_quote: Children with complications require stabilization first; feeding and fluids must be cautious because myocardial reserve and electrolyte handling are impaired. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154448/)
document_key_topics: Monitoring and emergency care in SAM
